mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments, making it ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development. It simplifies the installation process by packaging all necessary components into a single binary, but may lack some advanced features found in full Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the K3s binary
steps.description:
This step installs the K3s binary on your system, which is required to run the lightweight Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

```

> This command downloads and installs the latest K3s version.

steps.title: Verify K3s installation
steps.description:
Check if K3s is running properly by verifying the service status.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> Ensure the K3s service is active and running.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig file to interact with your K3s cluster using kubectl.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=/etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

chmod 600 /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml

```

> This sets the kubeconfig environment variable for kubectl.
> Ensure the kubeconfig file has the correct permissions.

steps.title: Verify nodes in the cluster
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your K3s cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Metrics Server
steps.description:
Install the Metrics Server to enable resource metrics for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml

```

> This command deploys the Metrics Server to your K3s cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This step removes K3s from your system. Be aware that this will delete all resources in the cluster and may leave orphaned resources.

```bash

/usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> Run this script to uninstall K3s.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your cluster data. This step will upgrade K3s to the latest version.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command upgrades K3s to the latest version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The K3s service may not be started or has failed.

**Fix:**
Run 'systemctl start k3s' to start the service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KUBECONFIG environment variable points to the correct file.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl get nodes' shows no nodes
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** K3s may not be running or the nodes are not registered.

**Fix:**
Check the K3s service status with 'systemctl status k3s'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The container image may not be available or the node lacks internet access.

**Fix:**
Check your internet connection and ensure the image is available in the specified registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new CNI plugins, improved handling of resource limits.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy storage backends.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new CNI configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with their statuses as 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements for running K3s.
